questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the length of a copper wire is increased by twice, then its resistivity will be
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 3: Same.

• Resistivity (ρ) is an intrinsic property of the material—it doesn’t depend on the wire’s length or cross-section.

• Only the resistance

$$R = \rho\,\frac{L}{A}$$

changes when you alter length (L) or area (A).

• Doubling the length doubles the resistance, but ρ itself remains unchanged.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following forces is/are fundamental in nature?

1. Gravitational force

2. Electromagnetic forces

3. Strong and weak nuclear forces

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
In the realm of physics, fundamental forces, also known as fundamental interactions, are the basic forces that govern the interactions between particles and objects. There are four recognized fundamental forces in nature :

1. Gravitational Force: This is the force of attraction that acts between all masses. Despite being the weakest of the four forces, it has an infinite range and is always attractive. It's described by Newton's law of gravitation and Einstein's general theory of relativity.

2. Electromagnetic Forces: These forces act between charged particles and are described by Coulomb's law and Maxwell's equations. They have an infinite range and include electric forces (between stationary charges) and magnetic forces (between moving charges).

3. Strong Nuclear Force: This is the force that holds the protons and neutrons together in an atom's nucleus. Despite its extremely short range (approximately the size of an atomic nucleus, $$10^{-15}$$ meters), it is the strongest of the four fundamental forces.

4. Weak Nuclear Force: This force is responsible for certain types of particle decay processes, such as beta decay in radioactive atoms. Its range is even smaller than that of the strong nuclear force, approximately $$10^{-18}$$ meters, and it is weaker than both the strong nuclear and the electromagnetic forces.

Given this information, the correct answer is :

Option D : 1, 2 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A wooden box of mass 2 kg and dimensions (30 cm × 15 cm × 10 cm) is placed on a table with sides 30 cm and 10 cm touching the tabletop. Which one of the following is the approximate pressure exerted on the table?
options: 111.1 N/m2
222.2 N/m2
333.3 N/m2
666.6 N/m2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following graphs correctly represents the current (I) - voltage (V) variation for a rectangular piece of a semiconductor wafer?
questions.solution: The current flowing through the semiconductor is not linearly proportional to the voltage. In forward bias the current increases by increasing the voltage in parabolic way.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: 'Beats' is a phenomenon that occurs when frequencies of two harmonic waves are
questions.solution:
The phenomenon of "beats" occurs when the frequencies of two harmonic waves are nearly the same.

When two waves of slightly different frequencies interfere with each other, they create a phenomenon known as beats. The resulting sound frequency that is heard is the average of the two frequencies, while the amplitude of the sound oscillates at the difference of the two frequencies. The beat frequency can be expressed mathematically as:

$$ f_{\text{beat}} = |f_1 - f_2| $$

where $ f_1 $ and $ f_2 $ are the frequencies of the two waves. This oscillation in amplitude leads to a pulsing effect, which is the characteristic sound associated with beats.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The magnetic field inside a current carrying very long solenoid is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 1: Uniform

For a very long current-carrying solenoid, the magnetic field inside is nearly the same at all points inside it.

According to NCERT, the magnetic field lines inside a long solenoid are:

  • parallel

  • equally spaced

  • directed along the axis of the solenoid

This shows that the magnetic field inside is uniform.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Uniform}}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Given below are the four cases in which certain heat transfer is taking place:

1. Ice is melting in a glass full of water

2. Water is boiling in an open container

3. A metal rod is heated in a furnace

4. A cup of coffee is allowed to cool on a table

In which of the above cases, the Newton's Law of Cooling is applicable ?
questions.solution:
Newton’s law of cooling applies when a body (with uniform temperature) exchanges heat by convection (or convection + radiation) with its surroundings, without any phase change. In formula form:

$$\frac{dT}{dt}=-k\bigl(T(t)-T_\infty\bigr)$$

where

• $T(t)$ is the object’s temperature at time $t$,

• $T_\infty$ is the ambient temperature,

• $k$ is a positive constant (proportional to the heat‐transfer coefficient).

Checking each case:

Ice melting in water

– Phase change at constant $0^\circ$C → temperature doesn’t change, so the simple $dT/dt$ law doesn’t apply.

Water boiling in an open container

– Phase change at $100^\circ$C → again constant temperature during boiling.

Metal rod heated in a furnace

– Often involves conduction along the rod and radiation at high temperatures; temperature is not uniform and there’s more than simple convection.

Cup of coffee cooling on a table

– No phase change, coffee cools by convection/radiation, roughly uniform temperature → fits Newton’s law.

Answer: Only case 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Escape speed from the Earth is close to 11.2 km s⁻¹. On another planet whose radius is half of the Earth's radius and whose mass density is four times that of the Earth, the escape speed in km s⁻¹ will be close to :
questions.solution:
To find the escape speed from another planet given its radius and mass density in relation to Earth's, we need to use the concept of escape velocity. The escape velocity $ v_e $ from a spherical body is given by:

$$ v_e = \sqrt{\frac{2 G M}{R}} $$

where:

  • $ G $ is the gravitational constant
  • $ M $ is the mass of the planet
  • $ R $ is the radius of the planet

We know the escape speed from Earth is 11.2 km/s. Therefore:

$$ v_{e, \text{Earth}} = 11.2 \text{ km/s} $$

We denote the escape velocity of the new planet as $ v_{e, \text{planet}} $. Let's express the mass of the new planet in terms of Earth's mass.

The mass $ M $ of a planet can be written as the product of its volume and density:

$$ M = \rho V $$

For a spherical body:

$$ V = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 $$

Therefore:

$$ M \propto \rho R^3 $$

Given the new planet's radius $ R_p $ is half of Earth's radius $ R_e $:

$$ R_p = \frac{R_e}{2} $$

Also, its density $ \rho_p $ is four times the density of Earth $ \rho_e $:

$$ \rho_p = 4 \rho_e $$

The new planet's mass $ M_p $ compared to Earth's mass $ M_e $ can be written as:

$$ M_p \propto \rho_p R_p^3 = 4 \rho_e \left(\frac{R_e}{2}\right)^3 = 4 \rho_e \left(\frac{R_e^3}{8}\right) = \frac{\rho_e R_e^3}{2} = \frac{M_e}{2} $$

Thus:

$$ M_p = \frac{M_e}{2} $$

Now we substitute $ M_p $ and $ R_p $ into the escape velocity formula:

$$ v_{e, \text{planet}} = \sqrt{\frac{2 G M_p}{R_p}} = \sqrt{\frac{2 G \frac{M_e}{2}}{\frac{R_e}{2}}} = \sqrt{\frac{2 G M_e}{R_e}} = \sqrt{\frac{G M_e}{R_e}} $$

This is because the factor of 2 in the denominator of the mass term and the factor of 2 in the radius term cancels out. So:

$$ v_{e, \text{planet}} = \sqrt{2} v_{e, \text{Earth}} $$

$$ v_{e, \text{planet}} = \sqrt{2} \cdot 11.2 \text{ km/s} $$

Calculating this value:

$$ v_{e, \text{planet}} = 11.2 \sqrt{2} \approx 11.2 \cdot 1.414 = 15.8 \text{ km/s} $$

Thus, the correct option is:\p>

Option B

15.8

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A spherical shell of outer radius R and inner radius R/2 contains a solid sphere of radius R/2 (see figure). The density of the material of the solid sphere is ρ and that of the shell is ρ/2. What is the average mass density of the larger sphere thus formed?
questions.solution:
To determine the average mass density of the larger sphere formed by a spherical shell with an outer radius $ R $ and an inner radius $ R/2 $, containing a solid sphere of radius $ R/2 $, follow these steps:

The density of the solid sphere is $ \rho $, while the density of the shell is $ \rho/2 $. The average density can be computed using the formula:

$ \text{Average density} = \frac{\text{Total mass}}{\text{Total volume}} $

Breaking this down:

  1. Volume of the solid sphere (inner part):

$ V_1 = \frac{4}{3} \pi \left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^3 $

  1. Volume of the outer shell:

$ V_{\text{shell}} = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 - V_1 $

  1. Volume of the entire larger sphere (outer radius $ R $):

$ V = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 $

  1. Mass of the solid sphere:

$ m_1 = \rho \times V_1 $

  1. Mass of the shell:

$ m_{\text{shell}} = \frac{\rho}{2} \times V_{\text{shell}} $

Now, substitute these into the average density formula:

$ \text{Average density} = \frac{m_1 + m_{\text{shell}}}{V} $

Calculating step-by-step:

  1. Calculate $ V_1 $:

$ V_1 = \frac{4}{3} \pi \left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^3 = \frac{4}{3} \pi \frac{R^3}{8} $

  1. Calculate $ V_{\text{shell}} $:

$ V_{\text{shell}} = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 - \frac{4}{3} \pi \frac{R^3}{8} = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 \left(1 - \frac{1}{8}\right) = \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 \frac{7}{8} $

  1. Calculate $ m_1 $:

$ m_1 = \rho \times \frac{4}{3} \pi \frac{R^3}{8} $

  1. Calculate $ m_{\text{shell}} $:

$ m_{\text{shell}} = \frac{\rho}{2} \times \left( \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 \frac{7}{8} \right) $

Combining these:

$ \text{Average density} = \frac{\rho \times \frac{4}{3} \pi \frac{R^3}{8} + \frac{\rho}{2} \times \frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 \frac{7}{8}}{\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3} $

Simplify the equation:

$ = \frac{\rho \frac{R^3}{8} + \frac{\rho}{2} \times \frac{7 R^3}{8}}{R^3} $

$ = \frac{\rho}{8} + \frac{\rho}{2} \times \frac{7}{8} = \frac{\rho}{8} + \frac{7 \rho}{16} $

Combine the terms:

$ = \frac{2 \rho + 7 \rho}{16} = \frac{9 \rho}{16} $

Thus, the average mass density of the larger sphere formed is $\frac{9\rho}{16}$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A microscope may be a combination of :
options: a convex lens and a convex mirror.
questions.solution:
A microscope is typically a combination of two convex lenses. The basic design of a simple microscope, also known as a magnifying glass, uses a single convex lens. However, compound microscopes, which are more common in scientific applications, use two convex lenses arranged in series to achieve higher magnifying power.

Each lens in a compound microscope serves a specific purpose:

Objective Lens: This is the lens closest to the object being viewed. It gathers light from the specimen and creates a enlarged real image.

Eyepiece Lens (Ocular Lens): This lens is closest to the observer's eye. It magnifies the real image created by the objective lens to create a larger virtual image that the observer can see.

The arrangement of these two lenses allows for greater magnification and resolution compared to a single lens. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: two convex lenses.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following optical phenomena supports that the light is a transverse wave?
questions.solution:
The phenomenon that supports the idea that light is a transverse wave is Option D: Polarization.

To understand why polarization is indicative of light being a transverse wave, let's delve into the concept a bit more:

Light, as an electromagnetic wave, consists of oscillating electric and magnetic fields that are perpendicular to each other and to the direction of wave propagation. In the case of a transverse wave, the oscillations occur in a direction perpendicular to the direction of the wave's advance.

Polarization refers to the orientation of the oscillations of the light wave. When light passes through a polarizing filter, the filter only allows waves oscillating in a particular direction to pass through. This demonstrates that light waves have orientations perpendicular to their direction of travel, which is a key characteristic of transverse waves.

To summarize why the other options are not indicative of light being a transverse wave:

Option A: Refraction: A phenomenon where light changes direction when it passes from one medium to another due to a change in speed. This is related to the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is transverse.

Option B: Diffraction: The bending of light waves around obstacles and the spreading of light waves when they pass through small openings. While this is also a wave property, it is not specific to light being transverse.

Option C: Interference: This is the phenomenon where two or more light waves superpose to form a resultant wave. It demonstrates the wave nature of light but does not specifically indicate that light is a transverse wave.

Therefore, polarization is the phenomenon that confirms the transverse nature of light waves, as it directly shows that light has specific orientations of vibration perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

The mathematical representation for polarized light can be expressed if one considers the electric field vector $$\vec{E}$$ which oscillates in a specific plane, indicating transverse wave characteristics:

$$\vec{E}(z,t) = E_0 \cos(kz - \omega t + \phi) \hat{e}$$

where:

  • $$E_0$$ is the amplitude of the electric field
  • $$k$$ is the wave number
  • $$\omega$$ is the angular frequency
  • $$\phi$$ is the phase constant
  • $$\hat{e}$$ is the unit vector indicating the direction of polarization

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The refractive index of crown glass is close to 3/2. If the speed of light in air is c, then the speed of light in the crown glass will be close to
questions.solution:
The refractive index ($ n $) of a medium is defined as the ratio of the speed of light in a vacuum ($ c $) to the speed of light in the medium ($ v $). Mathematically, this is expressed as:

$$ n = \frac{c}{v} $$

Given that the refractive index of crown glass is approximately $ \frac{3}{2} $, we can write:

$$ \frac{3}{2} = \frac{c}{v} $$

To find the speed of light in crown glass, we need to solve for $ v $. Rearranging the equation gives:

$$ v = \frac{c}{\frac{3}{2}} = \frac{2c}{3} $$

Thus, the speed of light in crown glass is $ \frac{2}{3}c $.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C: $ \left(\frac{2}{3}\right)c $

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the mass of a material, whose specific heat capacity is 400 J/(kg °C) for a rise in temperature from 15°C to 25°C, when heat received is 20 kJ ?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Two resistances of 5.0 Ω and 7.0 Ω are connected in series and the combination is connected in parallel with a resistance of 36.0 Ω. The equivalent resistance of the combination of three resistors is
questions.solution:
Step 1: Write down what is given.

  • Two resistances $R_1 = 5.0 \, \Omega$ and $R_2 = 7.0 \, \Omega$ are connected in series.

  • Their series combination is connected in parallel with $R_3 = 36.0 \, \Omega$.

Step 2: Find the series resistance of $R_1$ and $R_2$.

$ R_{\text{series}} = R_1 + R_2 = 5.0 + 7.0 = 12.0 \, \Omega $

Step 3: Now, $R_{\text{series}} = 12.0 \, \Omega$ is in parallel with $R_3 = 36.0 \, \Omega$.

For two resistors in parallel:

$ \frac{1}{R_{\text{eq}}} = \frac{1}{R_{\text{series}}} + \frac{1}{R_3} $

$ \frac{1}{R_{\text{eq}}} = \frac{1}{12} + \frac{1}{36} $

$ \frac{1}{R_{\text{eq}}} = \frac{3 + 1}{36} = \frac{4}{36} = \frac{1}{9} $

Step 4: Take reciprocal to get the equivalent resistance.

$ R_{\text{eq}} = 9.0 \, \Omega $

Final Answer: Option 3 → 9.0 Ω

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: For a human eye, where u is the distance of an object from the eye, f is the focal length of the lens and v is the distance of image from the eye, which is the correct schematic graph?
questions.solution: When object distance increase, in order to maintain image at retina, focal length increases initially, later for extreme distance it becomes constant. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is primarily responsible for conduction of current in a metal?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option B

Free electrons

To understand this, let's delve into the nature of electrical conduction in metals. Metals are characterized by their unique atomic structure, which includes a lattice of positively charged ions surrounded by a 'sea' of free electrons. These free electrons are not bound to any particular atom and are free to move throughout the metal. This freedom of movement is a critical property that allows metals to conduct electric current efficiently.

When an electric field is applied to a metal, these free electrons are influenced by the field and start drifting in the direction opposite to the field. This movement of free electrons constitutes the electric current. The bound electrons, on the other hand, are tightly held by their respective atoms and cannot move freely to conduct current. Ions in a metal lattice are also stationary and do not contribute to the conduction of current. Therefore, it is primarily the free electrons that are responsible for the conduction of current in a metal.

For a quantitative understanding, we can refer to Ohm's Law, which states:

$$V = IR$$

Where:

$$V$$ is the voltage across the conductor,

$$I$$ is the current through the conductor,

$$R$$ is the resistance of the conductor.

In the context of metals, the low resistance is largely due to the high density of free electrons that can move with minimal opposition, thus allowing a significant current to flow with a small applied voltage.

Therefore, free electrons play the crucial role in conducting current in metallic materials.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: An infinite combination of resistors, each having resistance $R = 4 \Omega$, is given below. What is the net resistance between the points A and B? (Each resistance is of equal value, $R = 4 \Omega$)
questions.solution:
Equivalent Resistance,

$$ \begin{array}{rlrl} y =\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{R}|| y \\\\ \Rightarrow y =\mathrm{R}+\frac{\mathrm{R} \times y}{\mathrm{R}+y} \\\\ \Rightarrow y =\frac{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{R} y+\mathrm{R} y}{\mathrm{R}+y} \\\\ \Rightarrow \mathrm{R} y+y^2 =\mathrm{R}^2+2 \mathrm{R} y \\\\ \Rightarrow y^2-4 y-16 =0 \\\\ \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}} \text { or } y =2+2 \sqrt{5} \text { ohm } \end{array} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Two resistors R1 and R2 arranged in parallel combination in an electrical closed circuit are made of the same material and of the same thickness. If the length of R2 is twice the length of R1, then the total resistance R satisfies

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following sketches correctly describes a lever of second class?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Lever of Second Class

A lever of the second class has three main parts:

  • The fulcrum is at one end. The fulcrum is the point where the lever pivots or turns.
  • The load (the thing you want to move or lift) is in the middle.
  • The effort (where you push or pull) is at the other end, opposite the fulcrum.

In a second-class lever:

  • The load is always between the fulcrum and where you apply effort.
  • The arm where you apply effort (effort arm) is always longer than the part holding the load (load arm), so you get a mechanical advantage greater than 1. This means it's easier to lift something.
  • Examples of second-class levers are wheelbarrows, nutcrackers, and bottle openers.

EXPLANATION:

When you look at sketches of different levers:

  • A second-class lever should always have:
    • Fulcrum at one end,
    • Load in the middle,
    • And effort at the other end.
  • The correct sketch must show the load between the fulcrum and the effort.

So, the sketch that correctly shows a lever of the second class is Option 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The length of a simple pendulum is increased four times to its previous value while the mass is doubled. What is the ratio of the new and previous time period of the pendulum?
questions.solution:
Calculation:

The time period of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$ T_{\text{old}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

If the length $ L $ of the pendulum is increased four times and the mass is doubled, we note that the time period is independent of the pendulum's mass. Therefore, the time period depends only on the length and gravitational acceleration.

For the new pendulum:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{4L}{g}} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{4}\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2 \times 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

Thus, comparing the initial and new time periods:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2T_{\text{old}} $

The ratio of the new to the old time period is:

$ T_{\text{new}} : T_{\text{old}} = 2 : 1 $

Therefore, the ratio of the new and previous time periods is 2:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A current of 0.6 A is drawn by an electric bulb for 10 minutes. Which one of the following is the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit?
questions.solution:
To determine the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit, we can use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The equation we will use is:

$$Q = I \cdot t$$

where:

  • $ Q $ is the electric charge in coulombs (C).
  • $ I $ is the current in amperes (A).
  • $ t $ is the time in seconds (s).

Given:

  • Current $ I = 0.6 $ A
  • Time $ t = 10 $ minutes

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds:

$$ t = 10 \text{ minutes} \times 60 \text{ seconds/minute} = 600 \text{ seconds} $$

Now we can substitute $ I $ and $ t $ into the equation:

$$ Q = 0.6 \text{ A} \times 600 \text{ s} = 360 \text{ C} $$

Therefore, the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit is 360 C. Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: 360 C

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: We hear an echo due to
questions.solution:
Concept:

Echo: An echo occurs when a sound wave reflects off a surface and is heard a short time after the original sound. This delay is caused by the time it takes for the sound wave to travel to the surface and back.

Reflection of sound: When sound waves encounter a hard surface, they bounce back, producing a reflected sound wave.

Conditions for Echo Formation:

The distance between the sound source and the reflecting surface should be at least 17.2 meters, assuming the speed of sound is approximately 344 m/s.

The reflecting surface should be large and hard to effectively reflect the sound.

Therefore, the phenomenon of hearing an echo is explained by the reflection of sound waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following telescopes contains only mirrors?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option C: Newtonian telescope

A Newtonian telescope is a type of reflecting telescope invented by Sir Isaac Newton. It uses a concave primary mirror to gather light and reflect it to a flat secondary mirror, which then reflects the light to an eyepiece positioned on the side of the telescope. This design does not include any lenses, relying solely on mirrors for its operation.

Here are brief descriptions of the other options for comparison:

Option A: Galilean telescope

A Galilean telescope is a type of refracting telescope named after Galileo Galilei. It uses a convex objective lens and a concave eyepiece lens. This design does not use mirrors but instead two lenses to focus light and form an image.

Option B: Keplerian telescope

A Keplerian telescope, also known as a Keplerian refractor, is similar to the Galilean telescope but uses two convex lenses. The objective lens gathers light and the eyepiece lens magnifies the image. Like the Galilean telescope, it uses lenses rather than mirrors.

Option D: Schmidt telescope

A Schmidt telescope, also known as a Schmidt camera, uses a combination of a spherical primary mirror and a corrector plate (lens) to correct spherical aberrations. Although it primarily relies on a mirror, it also incorporates a lens, therefore it does not consist entirely of mirrors.

Thus, the Newtonian telescope is the only type among the given options that uses only mirrors.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without changing the temperature, is called
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: specific latent heat.

Explanation:

The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without any change in temperature is called specific latent heat of vaporisation.

In NCERT terms:

  • Latent heat: Heat absorbed or released during change of state.

  • Specific latent heat: Heat required for unit mass of a substance to change its state without change in temperature.

So here, since the change is from liquid to vapour, it is specific latent heat.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Correct option} = \text{Option 2} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: An object is placed between infinity and the pole (P) of a convex mirror. The position of the image is
options: between pole (P) and the focus (F), behind the mirror
between the focus (F) and infinity, behind the mirror
between the pole (P) and the infinity, in front of the mirror
at the focus (F), behind the mirror
questions.solution:
For a convex mirror the mirror‐formula

$$\frac1u + \frac1v = \frac1f$$

applies, with

$u>0$ (object in front of mirror),

$f<0$ (convex mirror focal length),

$v<0$ (image behind mirror).

If $u\to\infty$, then $1/v=1/f$ ⇒ $v=f$ (image at the focus, behind).

As $u$ decreases from ∞ toward 0, $\;1/v=1/f-1/u$ becomes more negative ⇒ $|v|$ decreases from $|f|$ toward 0.

So for any finite object distance the image lies between the pole $P$ and the focus $F$, behind the mirror.

Answer: Option 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Airbags work on the principle of a chemical reaction triggered by the impact producing a gaseous product that causes a sudden volume change. Which one among the following chemical conversions is responsible for this?
options: Solid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide
Sudden conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option A: Sodium azide into nitrogen gas

Airbags typically use the rapid decomposition of sodium azide (NaN₃) to produce nitrogen gas (N₂), which inflates the airbag. The chemical reaction involved is:

$$ 2 \text{NaN}_3 \rightarrow 2 \text{Na} + 3 \text{N}_2 \uparrow $$

This reaction produces a large volume of nitrogen gas very quickly, causing the airbag to inflate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Buckminster fullerene, which looks like a football, is an allotropic form of
questions.solution:
Buckminster fullerene, also known as C60, is a spherical molecule made entirely of carbon atoms arranged in a pattern that resembles a soccer ball (football). The structure consists of 60 carbon atoms forming 12 pentagons and 20 hexagons, creating a highly stable and symmetrical shape. This particular molecular form of carbon falls under the group of compounds known as fullerenes, which are allotropes of carbon.

In chemistry, allotropes are different structural forms of the same element, where atoms are bonded together in different ways. For carbon, other notable allotropes include diamond, graphite, and graphene. Buckminster fullerene is an exciting form due to its unique properties and potential applications in materials science and nanotechnology.

Given the options:

Option A: Phosphorus

Option B: Sulfur

Option C: Carbon

Option D: Tin

The correct answer is Option C: Carbon.

Thus, Buckminster fullerene is an allotropic form of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not an example of a redox reaction?

questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct reactivity series with water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Copper sulphate crystals available in the market are blue coloured crystals. By careful heating, they turn to white colour. Which one of the following is responsible for the blue colour?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Water. Here's why:

Copper Sulfate Pentahydrate (CuSO₄·5H₂O): The blue crystals you see are actually copper sulfate pentahydrate. This means each copper sulfate molecule (CuSO₄) is bonded to five water molecules. These water molecules are responsible for the blue color.

Dehydration: When you heat the crystals, the water molecules are driven off (dehydration). The remaining copper sulfate (CuSO₄) is white.

The chemical equation for this process is:

$$CuSO₄·5H₂O \xrightarrow{heat} CuSO₄ + 5H₂O$$

Let me know if you have any more questions!

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the maximum number of electrons that can occupy the first shell of an atom?
questions.solution:
The maximum number of electrons that can occupy the first shell of an atom is 2.

This is determined by a general rule in atomic structure: the maximum number of electrons a principal energy shell can hold is given by the formula $$2n^2$$ where 'n' represents the principal quantum number (or the shell number).

For the first shell:

$n = 1$

Plugging this into the formula, we get: $$2 \times (1)^2 = 2 \times 1 = 2$$

So, the first shell can hold a maximum of 2 electrons!

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following salts is responsible for temporary hardness of water ?
questions.solution:
Temporary hardness of water is caused by the bicarbonates of calcium and magnesium.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: Bicarbonates of magnesium

Reason:

  • Temporary hardness is due to soluble bicarbonates.

  • Permanent hardness is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium.

Therefore, magnesium bicarbonate is responsible for temporary hardness of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Consider the following reaction:

2HgO $ \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} $ 2Hg + O2

The respective state of HgO, Hg and O2 in the above reaction is
questions.solution:
The reaction in question is the thermal decomposition of mercury(II) oxide (HgO) into elemental mercury (Hg) and oxygen gas (O2). The respective states of each of these substances are as follows:

Mercury(II) oxide (HgO) is a solid at room temperature. When it decomposes upon heating, it forms elemental mercury, which is a liquid at room temperature, and oxygen gas.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option D

Solid, liquid, gas

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following elements is polyatomic ?
questions.solution:
1. Polyatomic element: an element whose molecules consist of more than two atoms.

  2. Common elemental forms:

  • Sodium: metallic lattice (atoms not in discrete molecules)

  • Aluminium: metallic lattice

  • Chlorine: diatomic molecules ($Cl_2$)

  • Sulphur: typically octatomic molecules ($S_8$)

  1. Sulphur exists as $S_8$ rings, a molecule with eight atoms.

Answer: Option 2 (Sulphur) is polyatomic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a chemical change?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The relative atomic mass of boron (which exists in two isotopic forms 10B and 11B) is 10.81. What will be the abundance of 10B and 11B, respectively (consider a sample of 100 atoms)?
questions.solution:
To determine the abundance of the isotopes 10B and 11B, we can use the given information that the relative atomic mass of boron is 10.81 and that boron exists in two isotopic forms. Let's denote the abundance of 10B as $$x$$ (in percentage) and the abundance of 11B as $$100 - x$$ (in percentage), considering a sample of 100 atoms.

The formula for calculating the relative atomic mass (Ar) is:

$$ \text{Relative Atomic Mass} = \frac{\text{(Abundance of}\ ^{10}\text{B) (Mass of}\ ^{10}\text{B) + (Abundance of}\ ^{11}\text{B) (Mass of}\ ^{11}\text{B)}}{100} $$

Given:

Mass of 10B = 10

Mass of 11B = 11

Relative Atomic Mass = 10.81

Substituting the values, we have:

$$ 10.81 = \frac{x \times 10 + (100 - x) \times 11}{100} $$

Simplify the equation:

$$ 10.81 = \frac{10x + 1100 - 11x}{100} $$

$$ 10.81 = \frac{1100 - x}{100} $$

Multiplying both sides by 100 to eliminate the denominator:

$$ 1081 = 1100 - x $$

Solving for $$x$$:

$$ x = 1100 - 1081 $$

$$ x = 19 $$

Therefore, the abundance of 10B is 19%, and the abundance of 11B is 81%.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option A

19% and 81%

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Reaction of quick lime (CaO) with water to produce slaked lime (Ca(OH)<sub>2</sub>) is an example of
questions.solution:
The reaction of quick lime (CaO) with water to produce slaked lime (Ca(OH)$_2$) is an example of an exothermic reaction.

The chemical equation for this process is:

$$ \text{CaO} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{Ca(OH)}_2 + \text{Heat} $$

In this reaction, calcium oxide reacts with water, and heat is released during the formation of calcium hydroxide. This release of heat is characteristic of exothermic reactions, where energy is given off in the form of heat to the surroundings.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements with reference to the properties of water is not correct?
options: The specific heat of water is abnormally high.
Latent heat of fusion of water is very low.
Density of water is higher than ice.
Pure water is a non-conductor of electricity.
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement to determine which one is not correct regarding the properties of water:

Option A: The specific heat of water is abnormally high.

This statement is correct. Water has a very high specific heat capacity, meaning it requires a lot of energy to change its temperature. This property helps to stabilize the climate and is crucial for maintaining life.

Option B: Latent heat of fusion of water is very low.

This statement is not correct. The latent heat of fusion of water is actually quite high. Latent heat of fusion is the amount of heat required to convert a unit mass of a solid into a liquid without a temperature change. For ice, it is 334 kJ/kg, which is relatively high compared to many other substances.

Option C: Density of water is higher than ice.

This statement is correct. The density of liquid water is higher than that of ice. This is why ice floats on water. The unique structure of ice creates more open spaces between molecules, reducing its density.

Option D: Pure water is a non-conductor of electricity.

This statement is correct to an extent. Pure water (distilled water) is a poor conductor of electricity because it lacks ions. However, even small amounts of impurities can make water conductive.

Therefore, the statement that is not correct is:

Option B: Latent heat of fusion of water is very low.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: In electrolytic refining of copper, the electrolyte is a solution of
questions.solution:
The electrolyte used in the electrolytic refining of copper is a solution of acidified copper sulfate. During this process, impure copper is used as the anode, and pure copper sheets serve as the cathode. The copper sulfate solution allows for the transfer of copper ions from the anode to the cathode, resulting in the purification of copper.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: Acidified copper sulphate.

The reactions involved in this process are as follows:

At the Anode: Impure copper dissolves into the solution,

$$ \text{Cu (s)} \rightarrow \text{Cu}^{2+} \text{(aq)} + 2\text{e}^{-} $$

At the Cathode: Copper ions from the solution are deposited as pure copper,

$$ \text{Cu}^{2+} \text{(aq)} + 2\text{e}^{-} \rightarrow \text{Cu (s)} $$

This method ensures high purity of the copper extracted.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not used as a raw material in the manufacture of glass?
questions.solution:
Gypsum is not used as a raw material in the manufacture of glass.

Glass is typically made from the following raw materials:

Soda (Sodium carbonate, also known as soda ash): Serves as a flux to lower the melting temperature of the sand.

Silica (in the form of sand): The main ingredient, giving glass its basic structure.

Lime (calcium oxide or calcium carbonate): Acts as a stabilizing agent ensuring the durability of the glass.

Alumina: Used to enhance the chemical durability and mechanical strength of the glass.

Borax (Sodium borate): Sometimes used in specialty glasses to improve thermal and chemical resistance.

Gypsum, primarily composed of calcium sulfate, is typically used in construction (as drywall or plaster) rather than in glass production.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For manufacturing of glass, which among the following is used as a source of silica?
questions.solution:
For the manufacturing of glass, the source of silica used is:

Option D: Sand

Sand is primarily composed of silicon dioxide (SiO₂), which is the main ingredient in glass production. The silicon dioxide from sand melts at high temperatures and then cools to form glass.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following elements is known to be discovered the earliest?
questions.solution: Copper. Archaeological evidence shows native copper was in use by around 9000 BCE, well before gold (≈6000 BCE), oxygen (isolated in 1774), or uranium (identified in 1789).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: Diamond is hard due to extended covalent bonding in it
Fullerene is composed of five and six membered rings
Diamond is a good conductor of electricity
Graphite is used as a lubricant
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Diamond is a good conductor of electricity

Step-by-step explanation:

1. Diamond

In diamond, each carbon atom is bonded to four other carbon atoms by strong covalent bonds, forming a rigid three-dimensional network.

So:

  • Diamond is very hard

  • This is due to extended covalent bonding

  • But diamond does not conduct electricity, because it has no free electrons

So Option 1 is correct.

2. Fullerene

Fullerene is a form of carbon made up of five-membered and six-membered rings.

So Option 2 is correct.

3. Graphite

Graphite has layers of carbon atoms. These layers can slide over one another easily.

So:

  • Graphite is used as a lubricant

  • It also conducts electricity due to the presence of free electrons

So Option 4 is correct.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Bronze is an alloy of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Cu and Sn.

Here's why:

Bronze is a metal alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and tin (Sn). It's known for its durability, strength, and resistance to corrosion.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Option A: Cu and Zn - This describes brass, not bronze.
  • Option C: Zn and Mg - This is not a common metal alloy.
  • Option D: Fe and Cu - This combination is not typically used to make bronze.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Galvanization is a method of protecting iron from rusting by coating with a thin layer of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Zinc. Here's why:

Galvanization is a process where a thin layer of zinc is applied to iron or steel to prevent rust. This protection works due to the following principles:

  • Sacrificial Protection: Zinc is more reactive than iron. When exposed to the environment, zinc will oxidize (react with oxygen) before the iron. This means the zinc layer acts as a sacrificial anode, corroding instead of the iron, thus protecting the underlying metal.
  • Barrier Protection: The zinc coating forms a physical barrier that prevents oxygen and moisture from reaching the iron surface, which are essential for rust formation.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: Gallium: Gallium is a metal, but it's not commonly used for galvanization. It's primarily used in electronics and other specialized applications.
  • Option B: Aluminum: Aluminum can also provide some protection against corrosion, but it's not as effective as zinc for galvanization. Aluminum forms a very thin oxide layer that can be easily damaged, making it less protective than a zinc coating.
  • Option D: Silver: Silver is a noble metal, meaning it's resistant to corrosion. However, it's too expensive and not as effective as zinc for protecting iron in everyday applications.

In summary, zinc is the most effective and widely used metal for galvanization due to its sacrificial and barrier protection properties. This makes it the best choice for protecting iron from rust.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A liquid is heated up to a certain temperature. Which one of the following situation would correspond to the boiling of the liquid?
options: When atmospheric pressure becomes equal to the vapour pressure
When atmospheric pressure becomes less than vapour pressure

When atmospheric pressure becomes higher than the vapour
pressure
When vapour pressure becomes equal to the air pressure
questions.solution:
To determine which situation corresponds to the boiling of a liquid, let's delve into the concept of boiling. Boiling is defined as the point at which a liquid's vapor pressure equals the external or atmospheric pressure. When a liquid is heated, its molecules gain energy and its vapor pressure increases. Boiling occurs when the vapor pressure of the liquid equals the atmospheric pressure surrounding the liquid.

Keeping this definition in mind, let's analyze each option:

Option A: When atmospheric pressure becomes equal to the vapor pressure

This accurately describes the condition for boiling. At this equilibrium point, bubbles can form within the liquid because the vapor pressure inside the bubbles equals the surrounding atmospheric pressure.

Option B: When atmospheric pressure becomes less than vapor pressure

This situation indicates that the liquid could already be boiling. However, it does not specifically define the moment boiling begins.

Option C: When atmospheric pressure becomes higher than the vapor pressure

In this scenario, the liquid will not boil because the vapor pressure is not sufficient to overcome the atmospheric pressure.

Option D: When vapor pressure becomes equal to the air pressure

This description is essentially the same as Option A, assuming "air pressure" refers to the atmospheric pressure.

After evaluating all options, the correct answer is:

Option A: When atmospheric pressure becomes equal to the vapor pressure.

The mathematical representation of this condition can be formulated as:

$$ P_{\text{atmospheric}} = P_{\text{vapor}} $$

where $P_{\text{atmospheric}}$ is the atmospheric pressure and $P_{\text{vapor}}$ is the vapor pressure of the liquid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The chemical reaction : 2AgCl (s) → 2Ag (s) + Cl<sub>2</sub> (g) takes place
questions.solution:
1. Silver chloride, AgCl, is a photosensitive compound.

  2. Under sunlight, AgCl absorbs photons. This provides the energy needed to break the Ag–Cl bonds.

  3. The photochemical decomposition is represented as:

    $$2AgCl(s)\xrightarrow{\text{sunlight}}2Ag(s)+Cl_2(g)$$

  4. In the dark, on ordinary heating or under pressure, this reaction does not proceed appreciably.

Answer: Option 2 – in sunlight.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The number of structural isomers of pentane is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is 3.

Isomers:

  • Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers.
    • Therefore, the compounds that exhibit isomerism are known as isomers.
  • Such structural isomers, which differ in the chain of carbon atoms, are known as chain isomers.

Pentane:

  • Pentane is an organic compound that is a clear, colourless liquid.
  • The odour of pentane is similar to petroleum.
  • It is less dense than water and is insoluble in water. It is also known as quintane and n-pentane.
  • The rotatable bond count of pentane is 2.
  • Molecular Formula: C5H12.

Isomers of Pentane: There are three structural isomers of pentane:

  • n-pentane is the first isomer, which is a straight chain with a normal structure of pentane.

  • 2-methylbutane is the second isomer, which is a branched-chain structure in which one carbon atom is connected to three other carbon atoms.

  • 2,2-dimethylpropane is the third isomer, which is a branched-chain structure with the central carbon atom connected to four other carbon atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: C<sub>4</sub>H<sub>8</sub> belongs to the homologous series of
questions.solution:
To determine the homologous series to which the compound C4H8 belongs, we should first recognize that the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and cycloalkenes vary and can be used to classify the compound.

The general formulas are:

Alkanes: $$C_nH_{2n+2}$$

Alkenes: $$C_nH_{2n}$$

Alkynes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Cycloalkenes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Here, the compound C4H8 follows the formula for alkenes:

$$C_4H_8 \implies C_nH_{2n} \text{ where } n = 4$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Alkenes

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the chemical formula of Hypobromous acid?
questions.solution:
The chemical formula for Hypobromous acid is:

Option B: $$ \text{HOBr} $$

Hypobromous acid is a weak, unstable acid and is a bromine analog of hypochlorous acid. It can be represented as $$ \text{HOBr} $$, where the hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to an oxygen atom, which is in turn bonded to a bromine atom. This compound is typically formed by the reaction of bromine with water.
